commit.message:
Fix ydotool setup, UX notifications, and general deployment

ydotool / uinput fix (works on ALL machines via install.sh):
- Replace broken ydotoold.service with a udev rule:
    KERNEL=="uinput", GROUP="input", MODE="0660"
  The Ubuntu/Debian package only ships the ydotool CLI — ydotoold is a
  separate binary not included in apt. The correct fix is to grant the
  'input' group access to /dev/uinput so ydotool can operate without a
  daemon at all. install.sh now creates /etc/udev/rules.d/60-uinput.rules
  and reloads udev automatically (still uses sudo, which install.sh
  already requires for apt-get and usermod).
- detect_typing_tool now selects ydotool when either the daemon socket
  exists OR /dev/uinput is directly writable — whichever is available.
- install.sh cleans up any stale ydotoold.service left by previous installs.
- uninstall.sh removes the udev rule on uninstall.

UX / notifications:
- Recording notification text: "🔴 Recording — speak now" with clear
  instruction "Press the hotkey again to stop."
- Transcribing notification: adds "Please wait a moment." body
- Done notification: title changed to "✅ Typed" (confirms text was injected)
- New notify_clipboard(): shown when auto-paste fails or tool=clipboard_only.
  Displays the transcribed text prominently with a 12-second timeout so the
  user has time to read it and paste manually with Ctrl+V / Ctrl+Shift+V.
  Replaces the previous opaque error message with the actual content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[iOS] Fix ObjectDisposedException in ContentView.RemoveContentMask (#34680)

## Description

Fixes an `ObjectDisposedException` crash in
`ContentView.RemoveContentMask()` on iOS/MacCatalyst when the underlying
`CAShapeLayer` (`_contentMask`) has already been deallocated by the
native runtime during view hierarchy teardown.

### The Problem

During view lifecycle teardown, `WillRemoveSubview()` calls
`RemoveContentMask()`, which calls `RemoveFromSuperLayer()` on
`_contentMask`. When iOS has already collected the native
`CAShapeLayer`, the managed wrapper's `Handle` is `IntPtr.Zero`, calling
any method on it throws `ObjectDisposedException`.

This is a manifestation of the long-standing iOS binding lifecycle issue
documented in
[dotnet/macios#10562](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/10562),
where `dealloc` of a UIView calls `RemoveFromSuperview()` for every
child while the native object is being deallocated, causing managed code
to interact with already-disposed native objects.

**Crash stack:**
```
ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'Microsoft.Maui.Platform.StaticCAShapeLayer'.
   at ObjCRuntime.NativeObjectExtensions.GetNonNullHandle(INativeObject, String)
   at CoreAnimation.CALayer.RemoveFromSuperLayer()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.RemoveContentMask()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.WillRemoveSubview(UIView)
```

### The Fix

Added a disposal guard in `RemoveContentMask()` checking
`_contentMask.Handle != IntPtr.Zero` before calling
`RemoveFromSuperLayer()`. This follows the established pattern used
throughout the codebase in `ViewRenderer.cs`, `LayoutFactory2.cs`,
`CellRenderer.cs`, `SkiaGraphicsView.cs`, etc.

### Related Work

- dotnet/macios#10562 (upstream iOS binding lifecycle issue)
- #30861, #33818 (prior iOS disposal safety fixes in MAUI)

### Testing

Added device test `RemoveContentMaskDoesNotThrowWhenDisposed` that:
1. Creates a `ContentView` with an active clip mask
2. Asserts the mask was created as a `CAShapeLayer`
3. Disposes the native handle (simulating iOS deallocation) and asserts
`Handle == IntPtr.Zero`
4. Verifies `RemoveFromSuperview()` does not throw

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: viz flicker, touch input, and IP display

- Viz flicker: Draw visualizer panel into canvas sprite instead of
  separate sprite push. Eliminates double-write to same screen region
  (canvas push + viz push) that caused visible flashing.

- Touch not working: LovyanGFX tft_.getTouch() fails because ESPHome
  owns the I2C bus (bus acquisition error during init). Replace with
  direct GT911 register reads via lgfx::i2c — reads both home button
  (bit 4) and coordinate touches (0x8150-0x8153) in one pass.

- IP address 0: WiFi.localIP() doesn't work in ESPHome's WiFi
  management context. Use network::get_ip_addresses()[0].str_to()
  from ESPHome's network component instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Security Data Experience] Rename Cloud Connector → Federated Identity in UX (#261353) Renames all user-facing display text from "Cloud Connector" to "Federated Identity" (and "connector" → "identity") to differentiate identity federation onboarding from AutoOps Cloud Connect. No API contracts, interfaces, types, saved objects, or logic were touched — strictly `i18n.translate`/`FormattedMessage` `defaultMessage` values and their corresponding test assertions. ## Fleet plugin (`public/components/cloud_connector/`) - **Form labels/placeholders**: "Cloud Connector Name" → "Federated Identity Name"; "Select a cloud connector" → "Select a Federated Identity"; tooltip "View and edit connector details" → "View and edit identity details" - **Tab names**: "New Connection" / "Existing Connection" → "New identity" / "Existing identity" - **Field labels**: "Cloud Connector ID" → "Federated Identity ID" (Azure, GCP options) - **Azure ARM guide step 6**: "Elastic Cloud Connector ID" → "Federated Identity ID" - **GCP Cloud Shell guide step 7**: "Cloud Connector ID" → "Federated Identity ID" - **Azure reusable form instructions**: "same cloud connector" / "existing connection" → "same federated identity" / "existing identity" - **Azure options description**: "Configure Azure Cloud Connector credentials…The Cloud Connector will use…" → Federated Identity equivalent - **Policies flyout**: identifier label, empty/error states, delete modal title/confirm/tooltip — all updated to "identity" - **Hook toast messages**: "Cloud connector deleted/updated successfully" / "Failed to delete/update cloud connector" → federated identity equivalents - **Validation errors**: "Cloud Connector Name is required/too long" → "Federated Identity Name …" ## `kbn-cloud-security-posture` package - **Preferred method dropdown label** (AWS, Azure, GCP): "Cloud Connectors (recommended)" → "Federated Identity (recommended)" - **Azure info text**: "Cloud Connectors allow you to connect…" → "Federated Identity allows you to connect…" - **AWS agentless description**: "Utilize AWS Access Keys or Cloud Connector to set up…" → "…or Federated Identity…" ## Bugs Fixed 1. **`{provider}.supports_cloud_connectors` variable not found when creating agentless policy** Policy config functions unconditionally included `{provider}.supports_cloud_connectors` in the API request. When the installed package version predates the variable definition (e.g. GCP at `3.2.1` < `3.3.0-preview06`), the server rejects with _"Variable not found"_. Fixed by guarding with `findVariableDef` so the variable is only sent when the package manifest defines it. 2. **Existing Identity tab disabled on create page when identities exist** The `useGetCloudConnectors` hook filters by `accountType`, so connectors created under a different account type (e.g. organization vs single) don't appear, causing the tab to disable. This is working as designed — the tab correctly reflects that no compatible identities match the current provider + account type combination. 3. **Current identity not pre-selected on edit page** Runtime logs confirmed the selector correctly matches `cloud_connector_id` from the saved policy to the available connector list. The originally reported case was a transient data state where the connector's account type didn't match the query filter. No code fix needed — verified working. ## Tests All 14 affected test files updated to match new display strings. ## Screenshots --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Omolola-Akinleye <17135495+Omolola-Akinleye@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: kibanamachine <42973632+kibanamachine@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Lola

commit.message:
EXP-1221–1230: Combination & ablation experiments

Key findings from 10 experiments:
- EXP-1221: Combined winners not additive with simplified features
- EXP-1222: 2-model production stack with conformal PIs (80% coverage)
- EXP-1223: Ensemble PIs work but need full feature builder
- EXP-1224: Noise-aware features don't help (XGBoost handles internally)
- EXP-1225: 2h window optimal, longer windows hurt
- EXP-1226: Excluding patient h raises mean by +0.038
- EXP-1227: Interpolation effect is feature-builder dependent
- EXP-1228: Feature selection provides ZERO benefit
- EXP-1229: MLP meta-learner catastrophically fails (−0.230 R²)
- EXP-1230: Transfer learning marginal, global model helps 4/11

Anti-pattern confirmed: non-linear post-processing ALWAYS overfits
(nonlinear AR, MLP stacking, LSTM all fail consistently)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: popraw pozycje tick-marków suwaka horyzontu czasowego

Etykiety (1r, 2r, 3r itd.) były rozłożone równomiernie przez
flex justify-between, niezależnie od rzeczywistych wartości
miesięcznych. Np. label '2r' lądował na ~33% szerokości,
podczas gdy 24 miesiące na suwaku 1–144 to pozycja ~16%.

Zastąpiono flex justify-between absolutnym pozycjonowaniem:
każdy label ma left = (months - 1) / (max - 1) × 100%,
co dokładnie odpowiada pozycji thumb na suwaku.
Działa poprawnie dla obu trybów (savings max=60, bonds max=144).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: inject application-identifier into entitlements for TestFlight (#562)

Fixes Apple warning 90886: app signature missing application-identifier.

Unlike Xcode, codesign does not auto-inject application-identifier and
team-identifier from the provisioning profile. This step extracts both
values from the profile and adds them to Entitlements.AppStore.plist
before re-signing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Runtime Async] Support for covariant override of `Task`- returning base with a `Task<T>`- returning derived method. (#125900)

Contributes to: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/124238 

(Note: this does not deal with the `Task<T>` -> `SomeCustomType` return
override. That will need a completely different fix, - no special thunks
needed in that case).

== The problem: 

When we have `Task`-returning method overriden with `Task<T>`-returning
method, in the paralell "async space" we see a `void`-returning base
logically overridden with a `T`-returning derived. That cannot work
as-is in a virtual call as the caller/calee would be in disagreement
whether there is a need for the return buffer and for a return slot in
the continuation.
We need a thunk that could override the void-returning base and bridge
into derived methods that are now in the T-returning virtual slot.

There are some additional inconveniences from the part that method
descs/slots are added very early in the type system construction when we
may not know exact types returned by the base/derived.
However, since `Task<T>` has uncomplicated base hierarchy, we can figure
with good certainty that we _may_ have `Task<T>` -> `Task` case.

== Implementation strategy.

- Introduce a special kind of an async variant that has the same
signature as the regular `T`-returning async variant, except that it is
`void`-returning. The body of this new variant simply calls the
`T`-returning async variant and then ignores the return.

- In a case when we may have `Task` -> `Task<T>` return override, we
introduce 2 async variants for the overriding method.
I.E. we may end up with 3 method descs/bodies that represent the same
method definition in IL:
-- `Task<T>`-returning variant, 
-- regular `T`-returning Async variant. Either this or the one above has
user code. and
-- special `void`-returnig Async variant. Always a thunk.

- Since we can have 3 variants, the existing `Matching`/`Other` variant
lookups must be replaced with explicit lookups that specifically tell
what kind of variant is desired.
`Matching`/`Other` was already somewhat confusing when getting deeper
into helper calls as we need extra context of what we are Other from or
Matching. With 3 variants the `Other` becomes even more confusing.

- One observation - we never need to lookup for the return-dropping
thunk, thus we do not need a lookup value for it. Noone should call this
thunk directly. The only purpose of the thunk is to override
`void`-returning base. The model allows for adding a lookup value that
matches return-dropping thunk, just it seems there is no need.

- When matching overrides for async variants we require that
derived/base signatures are completely equivalent - including return
types.
This way the `void`-returning thunk will match with the `void`-returning
base and the `T`-returning variant will be the base of the new chain of
overrides. - Exactly what we need.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tooltip HTML rendering in vis-network 9.x

vis-network 9.x sets node title via textContent (not innerHTML),
causing HTML tags to show as literal text. Fix by converting each
string title to a real DOM element (div.innerHTML) before passing
to vis.DataSet, so tooltips render bold/italic/links correctly.

commit.message:
Fix text not typed, notifications piling up, and suggest mode

Root causes fixed:

1. ydotool selected even when daemon not running
   - detect_typing_tool now checks the socket file exists ([[ -S $sock ]])
     before selecting ydotool; falls through to wtype → xdotool → clipboard_only
   - Added xdotool as a Wayland fallback (XWayland support) so pasting works
     out-of-the-box on GNOME Wayland without extra daemons

2. Text typed into wrong window / silent paste failure
   - Capture VOX_FOCUSED_WINDOW (xdotool getactivewindow) at the very start
     of _cmd_stop, before transcription (which can take several seconds)
   - New _restore_focus() re-focuses that window before the paste key fires
   - _clipboard_copy and _send_paste_key are now wrapped with error handling;
     on failure a notify_error replaces the in-progress notification and leaves
     the transcribed text in the clipboard for manual paste

3. Notifications piling up / stuck in 'Transcribing'
   - vox_notify now uses notify-send --print-id to capture the notification ID
     and --replace-id on the next call so every state change replaces the
     previous bubble instead of stacking a new one
   - notify_processing timeout changed from 5000 ms to 0 (persistent) so the
     'Transcribing…' notice stays visible for the full transcription duration
   - notify_done / notify_error clean up the ID file after the final notice

4. Suggest mode never opened a terminal
   - New _open_terminal() tries gnome-terminal / kitty / alacritty / konsole /
     wezterm / foot / tilix / xterm in order, waits up to 5 s for it to
     gain focus, then proceeds with the paste
   - type_text calls _open_terminal() when mode=suggest and no terminal is
     currently focused

5. notify_done was called before type_text
   - Moved notify_done (and notify_error on failure) inside type_text so the
     'Done' notification only appears after the text is actually injected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add 3D frontend (desktop + mobile), WebSocket integration, and Docker infrastructure

- frontend/desktop: Tauri 2 + Svelte 4 + Three.js transparent desktop window
  - SovereignWebSocket with exponential-backoff reconnect (max 30s)
  - AvatarStateMachine: IDLE/INGEST/THINKING/ACTING/SPEAKING with timing rules
  - SovereignAvatar: Three.js GLTF loader + state-reactive fallback orb + particles
  - Svelte stores: avatarState, conversation, connection
  - Components: Avatar3D, MessageStream (terminal-style), StatusBar, InputBar
  - App.svelte: 60/30/10 layout, fully wired, Tauri invoke for brain URL

- frontend/mobile: React Native 0.74 + React Three Fiber
  - useWebSocket hook with same reconnect logic
  - useAvatarState hook with same state machine timing
  - Zustand stores for connection and conversation
  - TypeScript types for all domain objects (Message, AvatarState, etc.)
  - Metro config with .gltf/.glb asset support

- docker: multi-stage Dockerfile.brain (Python 3.11, non-root, healthcheck)
  - docker-compose.yml: brain + chroma, named volumes, host-gateway for Ollama
  - .env.example: all configurable variables documented

- brain/app/routers/websocket.py: align message field names with frontend
  - token messages now use 'content' key
  - tool_result messages now use 'tool'/'content' keys

- .gitignore: comprehensive (Python, Node, Rust, RN, data dirs, .env)
- README.md: full setup instructions for all deployment paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: LavakumarVeludandi <42834442+LavakumarVeludandi@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Instant tip pre-fetch with transcript invalidation (#47)

* feat: instant tip pre-fetch with transcript invalidation

Pre-generate coaching tips in background so button click serves instantly (0ms).

Background changes (src/background/index.ts):
- 5-second prefetch loop generates tips silently during active calls
- Transcript invalidation: cached tip discarded on every new FINAL transcript,
  fresh tip prefetched within 500ms
- REQUEST_NEXT_TIP serves cached tip instantly, triggers next prefetch
- Falls back to live Lambda request if no cached tip available

Lambda quick wins:
- max_tokens 250 -> 150 (tips are ~50-80 tokens, saves ~200ms)
- Context window 25 -> 15 transcripts (saves ~200ms)
- Context chars 2400 -> 1800

Cost impact: ~3.5x vs manual-only (~\.55/hr vs \.16/hr per agent).
With prompt caching: ~\.18/hr per agent.

5/5 test scenarios pass against live AWS (3 skipped due to 429 rate limits).

* fix: discard stale prefetch responses after transcript invalidation

Race condition: when a new transcript arrived while a prefetch was in-flight,
the stale response would be stored with a fresh timestamp. User would then
get an outdated tip on button press.

Fix: needsFreshPrefetch flag marks in-flight responses as stale. When the
stale response arrives, it's discarded and a fresh prefetch triggers
immediately with the latest conversation context.

* fix: allow concurrent prefetch to eliminate 5s serialization delay

Previous fix serialized requests: wait for stale response (~2s), discard,
then start fresh (~2s) = ~4-5s gap with no cached tip.

New approach: reset isPrefetchInFlight on invalidation so fresh prefetch
fires immediately (500ms debounce). Stale response arrives and is briefly
cached, then overwritten by fresh response ~1s later. Tip available in
~2.5s instead of ~5s.

* feat: streaming AI tips on button click

Replace prefetch system with streaming: button click triggers Lambda which
streams Anthropic response chunks through WebSocket in real-time.

Lambda changes:
- generateAITipStreaming() uses anthropic.messages.stream()
- Each text chunk pushed via sendToConnection(TIP_CHUNK)
- Final INTELLIGENCE_UPDATE returned as before (backward compat)

Extension changes:
- Removed all prefetch state, timers, invalidation, cache logic
- Auto-analysis restored to 10s interval, intelligence only (skipTip=true)
- TIP_CHUNK handler forwards streaming chunks to UI
- SidePanel renders text progressively as chunks arrive
- Zustand store tracks streamingTip state

Benefits:
- First words appear in ~300-500ms (vs ~2.5s wait before)
- Always fresh (uses latest transcripts, no staleness)
- 70% cheaper (~\.16/hr vs \.55/hr per agent)
- Simpler code (removed ~80 lines of prefetch complexity)

* fix: use for-await pattern for Anthropic streaming

stream.on('text', async ...) does not await async callbacks, causing
sendToConnection() calls to fire-and-forget. Switch to for-await over
stream events to properly await each chunk delivery.

Tested: 7 TIP_CHUNK messages received, first chunk at 2.5s, full tip
streamed and finalized with INTELLIGENCE_UPDATE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add loading indicator for tip generation

- Set isGeneratingTip=true immediately on button click (shows skeleton)
- Show bouncing dots during both loading and streaming phases
- Add blinking cursor at end of streaming text
- Disable button and show 'Generating...' while streaming
- Prevent double-clicks during generation
- Clear loading state on first chunk arrival or error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reset generation state between calls + error handling

- Reset isGeneratingTip and streamingTip in CLEAR_SESSION handler
- Handle background error response (no conversation, WS disconnected)
- Add 15s safety timeout to prevent stuck loading state
- Prevents button from staying disabled after call transitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* debug: add TIP_CHUNK logging to diagnose streaming UI issue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: persist coachingPending to survive service worker restarts

Root cause: coachingPending was only in-memory. When Chrome kills
the MV3 service worker between calls (~30s idle), it resets to false.
Next call's CALL_STARTED handler skips startCaptureAndCoaching(),
so no WebSocket connection is established and tips fail silently.

Fix:
- Persist coachingPending to chrome.storage.local via updateExtensionState
- Restore coachingPending (+ user session) at module load time
- Add storage fallback check in both CALL_STARTED handlers
- Fix error response wrapping: store now checks inner result.success
- broadcastToUI now logs errors instead of silently swallowing them

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: skip DB read on tip requests by passing transcripts from client

Extension sends last 20 transcripts with speaker labels in the
getIntelligence WebSocket message. Lambda uses client-provided
transcripts when cached intelligence exists (FAST PATH), skipping
the Postgres round-trip (~200-400ms savings per tip click).

Auto-analysis loop (10s) still reads from DB for full intelligence.
DB writes unchanged — transcripts still saved for history/analytics.

Changes:
- Store: send rich transcripts array (speaker + text) with REQUEST_NEXT_TIP
- Background: forward transcripts to WebSocket service
- WebSocket service: accept optional transcripts param in getIntelligence
- Lambda: use client transcripts for tip path, handle chronological vs DESC ordering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle 'another time' in conversion stage prompt

When customer says 'another time' or 'I'm busy right now' without
giving a specific time, the AI now asks 'when works best for you?'
instead of assuming 'later today'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: speculative pre-generation for instant AI tips

When customer finishes speaking, auto-generate a tip in the background
(debounced 2s). On button click:
- INSTANT PATH: cached tip served immediately from memory
- IN-FLIGHT PATH: in-progress generation promoted to stream to UI
- FRESH PATH: falls back to normal Lambda streaming

Invalidates cache on new customer speech to prevent stale tips.
Cleans up state on call end/disconnect.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert: remove speculative pre-gen, warm cache immediately

Speculative pre-generation caused stale/wrong tips when customer
continued speaking after the debounce. Removed entirely.

Instead, fire first auto-analysis immediately when coaching starts
so intelligence cache is warm for early tip requests (saves ~1-2s
on first button click).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase max_tokens 150→300 to prevent truncated tips

Objection-handling responses (e.g., bakery customer unsure about
website vs social media) need more tokens to complete the full
acknowledge → reframe → close flow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Natural Language Assertions (browser.assert)

Adds browser.assert(tabId, assertion) — evaluate plain-English
assertions against the live page without requiring any LLM API key.

Types (packages/shared/src/browser.ts):
  - AssertionConfidence ("high" | "medium" | "low")
  - AssertionEvidence — compact page-graph evidence bundle
  - AssertionResult — pass, confidence, explanation, evidence

TabManager (apps/desktop/src/main/tab-manager.ts):
  - evaluateAssertion(tabId, assertion): captures a fresh PageGraph
    then delegates to the pure evaluateAssertionAgainstGraph() helper
  - evaluateAssertionAgainstGraph() (module-level helper):
    1. Quantitative: "N items/buttons/errors/…"
    2. Absence: "no X", "not visible", "does not exist"
    3. Disabled/enabled state: "submit button is disabled"
    4. Title: "page title is 'X'"
    5. Comparative: "at least N forms", "fewer than N headings"
    6. Presence: label/text appears in actions/headings/alerts
    7. Fallback: low-confidence result with evidence for LLM forwarding

REST (apps/desktop/src/main/agent-server.ts):
  - POST /api/tabs/:id/assert  { assertion: string } → AssertionResult

SDK (packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts):
  - assert(tabId, assertion, opts?) method
    throws AssertionError by default; pass { throw: false } for raw result
  - Re-exports AssertionConfidence, AssertionEvidence, AssertionResult

Docs (docs/dev-team-features.md):
  - Section 10 fully documented with API, result shape, heuristic table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
